target_countries.recommended_role: Primary pilot: local mini-grid and e-cooker telemetry available
Replication candidate: extreme cooking gap, nearby market
Large-scale future market after infrastructure review
Large-market future expansion; grid constraints need separate design
Benchmark for large clean-cooking transition programs
Benchmark for refugee/host-community fuel transition lessons

pitch_brief.content: GridCook AI: a mini-grid aware clean-cooking planner for electric pressure cookers and compatible clean cooking appliances.

Clean-cooking access is the binding problem, not just appliance availability. Kenya has roughly 37.9 million people without clean cooking in the 2023 World Bank/SDG access data, and the 2022 WHO rural estimate is only about 10.4%.

Use smart-plug data, mini-grid telemetry, PV forecasts, and household preference signals to forecast cooking load, recommend clean-cooking windows, stagger appliance use, and give operators a dashboard for adoption, safety, and grid headroom.
MVP models: cooking-session detection, short-horizon load/PV forecast, household clustering, and a constrained scheduler that respects voltage, battery SOC, phase load, and meal-time preferences.

Start in Oloika-style Kenyan mini-grids with 50-100 households, using smart plugs and SMS/WhatsApp prompts. Compare fuel stacking, e-cooker kWh, peak load, voltage events, and self-reported time/health outcomes before and after scheduling.

After the pilot, replicate in nearby East African markets with high clean-cooking gaps: Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, and Ethiopia. Keep the model portable by using standard telemetry fields and a low-bandwidth phone interface.

Hackathon proof of concept: 2-4 days. MVP build: 8-10 weeks at roughly USD 75k-120k. Field pilot: 6 months at roughly USD 350k-600k for devices, field staff, training, data platform, and evaluation. Multi-site scale: 18-24 months at roughly USD 2m-5m.

Main risks are appliance affordability, fuel stacking, unreliable connectivity, low user trust, unsafe wiring, insufficient evening battery headroom, and behavior change fatigue. Mitigations are lease-to-own appliances, local champions, offline-first scheduling, safety checks, tariff incentives, and explicit opt-out control.
Fund a data-backed field pilot that proves whether AI-assisted scheduling can accelerate clean-cooking adoption without overloading mini-grids or pushing costs onto low-income households.

judge_mapping.evidence: Uses SDG 7.1.2 clean-cooking access gap; Kenya and Sub-Saharan Africa have large measured gaps.
Model is tied to real telemetry: smart-plug sessions, voltage, PV, battery SOC, and grid consumption.
Global unserved estimate is about 2.06 billion people; Kenya pilot TAM is about 37.9 million without clean cooking.
Oloika data shows e-cooking consumed only about 2.8% of June site load in the trial, so a staged scheduling pilot is plausible.
Workbook includes target-country ranking, e-cooker load summaries, mini-grid daily energy, and implementation assumptions.

key_metrics.metric: Oloika e-cooker energy in June trial
Oloika e-cooker energy per participating household
E-cooker trial share of mini-grid AC consumption

case_study_lessons.evidence: The UNHCR/WLPGA case succeeded because LPG suppliers, logistics, equipment procurement, and safety guidance were in place.
case_study_lessons.design_implication: Do not pitch AI alone. Pair the scheduler with appliance financing, local technicians, and operator training.

case_study_lessons.evidence: The case tracked firewood demand, kitchen air quality, food diversity, host-community effects, and safety.
case_study_lessons.design_implication: Pilot evaluation should track adoption, fuel stacking, voltage events, household time saved, and health/forest proxies.

case_study_lessons.evidence: The case highlighted local champions, site visits, and stakeholder understanding of the cooking technology.
case_study_lessons.design_implication: Use local champions and simple phone prompts; do not rely on a black-box app to change cooking routines.

case_study_lessons.evidence: The case reports firewood demand dropping from about 462,000 MT/year to about 37,000 MT/year after LPG introduction.
case_study_lessons.design_implication: Frame GridCook AI around avoided biomass demand and reduced peak-load risk, not just app engagement.

sources.used_for: WHO/SDG 7.1.2 clean cooking access, rural/urban 2022 values
World Bank clean fuels and technologies for cooking, total 2023 values
Smart-plug power, energy, voltage, and connection summaries
Mini-grid PV, consumption, battery SOC, and daily energy summaries
Implementation lessons from clean-cooking fuel transition in refugee camps

assumptions.assumption: People without clean cooking = population x (100 - clean-cooking access %) / 100, using 2023 access and 2023 population.
Cumulative smart-plug energy readings are treated as more reliable than irregular power integration for kWh totals.
A new cooking session starts after a power-data gap greater than 20 minutes; values below 100 W were already excluded in the source data.
AC consumption kWh is integrated from power telemetry with gaps over 30 minutes excluded.
Budget ranges are order-of-magnitude hackathon planning estimates, not vendor quotes.
